docs.title: TRICERATOPS
docs.text: Bayesian false-positive probability calculator for candidate exoplanets.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
from triceratops import ValidationWorkspace, LightCurve

ws = ValidationWorkspace(tic_id=237101326, sectors=[1, 2])

result = ws.compute_probs(light_curve=lc, period_days=5.0)
print(f"FPP = {result.fpp:.4f}")

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: See the API Reference for full documentation of the public interface.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This section documents the public Python API for TRICERATOPS.

Module Description ValidationWorkspace Main user-facing API for running validations ValidationEngine Stateless compute core Domain Types Core data types: stars, light curves, scenarios, results Configuration Config and MissionConfig Protocols Provider interfaces for dependency injection Validation Internals PreparedValidationInputs, ValidationPreparer, error types

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Frozen configuration objects controlling validation behavior.

docs.title: Config
docs.text:
Runtime parameters for a single TRICERATOPS+ validation run.

All parameters have scientifically-motivated defaults matching the original codebase. Override by constructing with explicit keyword arguments.

Attributes:

Name Type Description n_mc_samples int

Number of Monte Carlo draws per scenario (was N in original).

n_best_samples int

Number of top-likelihood draws to retain for best-fit parameter reporting (was N_samples = 1000 in the accumulation block).

seed int | None

Optional NumPy RNG seed. When set, compute runs are reproducible.

parallel bool

If True, use vectorized (masked array) likelihood evaluation. If False, use the serial per-sample loop. Original default: True.

flat_priors bool

If True, draw planet radii uniformly instead of using the broken-power-law distribution. Original parameter name: flatpriors.

mission str

Default mission when not overridden by MissionConfig.

docs.title: n_workers = 0 class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
Number of worker processes for scenario-level parallelism.

0 — serial execution (default; fully reproducible with np.random.seed). -1 — one worker per scenario up to os.cpu_count(). N — exactly N worker processes.

Note: parallel workers each receive an independent OS-entropy RNG seed. For fully reproducible parallel runs pass an explicit seed via np.random.seed() before calling calc_probs with n_workers=0, or use n_workers=0 and rely on Config.parallel=True for intra-scenario vectorisation.

docs.title: mission_config property
docs.text: Convenience access to the MissionConfig for self.mission.

docs.title: MissionConfig
docs.text:
Pixel geometry and timing parameters for a specific survey mission.

Do not instantiate directly -- use the for_mission() class method.

docs.title: pixel_size_deg property
docs.text: Pixel size converted to degrees.

docs.title: for_mission(mission) classmethod
docs.text:
Return the MissionConfig for the named mission.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default mission str

One of "TESS", "Kepler", or "K2".

required

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If mission is not a recognized name.

docs.title: Domain Types
docs.text: Core data types used throughout the TRICERATOPS library.

docs.title: Star
docs.text: One catalogued star in the photometric field.

docs.title: mag_for_band(band)
docs.text:
Return the magnitude for the given filter band, or None if unavailable.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default band str

One of "TESS", "J", "H", "K", "B", "V", "g", "r", "i", "z".

required

docs.title: StellarField
docs.text: All stars in the photometric search aperture, target at index 0.

docs.title: Invariants
docs.text:
• stars is non-empty.
  • stars[0].tic_id == target_id (target is always index 0).
  • No two stars share a TIC ID.

Use the mutation methods (add_neighbor, remove_neighbor, update_star) rather than mutating stars directly so that these invariants are maintained. Call validate() to check them explicitly before a compute job.

docs.title: stars_with_flux_data()
docs.text: Return stars that have a non-None, positive transit_depth_required.

docs.title: add_neighbor(star)
docs.text:
Append a neighbor star to the field.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If a star with the same TIC ID already exists.

docs.title: remove_neighbor(tic_id)
docs.text:
Remove a neighbor star by TIC ID.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If tic_id is the target star's ID.

ValueError

If no star with tic_id is found.

docs.title: update_star(tic_id, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Update fields on a star by TIC ID.

Accepts both direct Star attribute names and TIC-style aliases:

============ ================================== Alias Maps to ============ ================================== Teff stellar_params.teff_k mass stellar_params.mass_msun logg stellar_params.logg metallicity stellar_params.metallicity_dex ============ ==================================

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If no star with tic_id is found.

TypeError

If an alias update is requested but stellar_params is None.

AttributeError

If an unknown attribute name is given.

docs.title: validate()
docs.text:
Assert all structural invariants.

Called by ValidationPreparer.prepare() after catalog assembly and by ValidationEngine.compute_prepared() before compute. May also be called explicitly in debug/test flows.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If any invariant is violated.

docs.title: LightCurve
docs.text: A phase-folded, normalised photometric time series ready for model fitting.

docs.title: with_renorm(flux_ratio)
docs.text:
Return a new LightCurve renormalised to a single star's contribution.

This is the vectorised equivalent of renorm_flux() from funcs.py:225-238. flux_ratio is the fraction of aperture flux from the host star (0 < fr <= 1).

docs.title: ExternalLightCurve
docs.text: A ground-based follow-up observation in a specific photometric band.

docs.title: Ephemeris
docs.text:
Transit ephemeris parameters.

Attributes:

Name Type Description period_days float

Orbital period in days.

t0_btjd float

Transit midpoint in BTJD (BJD - 2,457,000). Must be < 10,000.

duration_hours float | None

Transit duration in hours, or None if unknown.

source str

Origin of this ephemeris ("manual", "exofop", etc.).

warnings tuple[str, ...]

Non-fatal issues from resolution.

docs.title: ResolvedTarget
docs.text:
A resolved astronomical target: identity plus optional ephemeris.

This is the output of EphemerisResolver.resolve() and the natural input to LightkurveRawSource and the artifact layer. It prevents TIC ID and ephemeris from being passed separately and getting out of sync. Ephemeris may be None for catalog-only or partial-assembly flows; LC prep entrypoints require it to be present.

docs.title: ScenarioID
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

All supported astrophysical scenarios. StrEnum so values are strings.

docs.title: planet_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text:
Scenarios that count as true planet detections for FPP.

FPP = 1 - sum(P(s) for s in planet_scenarios()).

Matches triceratops.py:1635: self.FPP = 1-(prob_df.prob[0]+prob_df.prob[3]+prob_df.prob[9]) where index 0=TP, 3=PTP, 9=DTP. STP (index 6) is NOT included — this matches the published TRICERATOPS methodology. Do not change.

docs.title: nearby_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text: Scenarios counted toward NFPP.

docs.title: trilegal_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text: Scenarios requiring TRILEGAL background population data.

docs.title: contrast_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text: Scenarios that use a contrast curve when available.

docs.title: twin_eb_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text: Base EB scenarios that also return a twin (q>=0.95) result.

docs.title: eb_scenarios() classmethod
docs.text: All EB scenarios including twin variants.

docs.title: ValidationResult
docs.text: Final output of a complete TRICERATOPS+ candidate validation run.

docs.title: get_scenario(scenario_id)
docs.text: Return the ScenarioResult for the given ID, or None if not found.

docs.title: ScenarioResult
docs.text:
Best-fit parameters and Bayesian evidence for one astrophysical scenario.

Replaces the raw dict returned by each lnZ_* function in marginal_likelihoods.py. Array fields have shape (n_best_samples,) -- the top-1000 draws by likelihood. Scalar fields set to zeros arrays for inapplicable scenario types (e.g., planet_radius is zero-filled for EB scenarios).

docs.title: StellarParameters
docs.text: Physical properties of a single star as used by scenario computations.

docs.title: from_tic_row(row) classmethod
docs.text:
Construct from a TIC catalog row dict. Fills defaults for NaN/None fields.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default row dict

dict with keys matching TIC column names (mass, rad, Teff, plx). Any field may be NaN or None; defaults will be substituted.

required

docs.title: ContrastCurve
docs.text:
AO or speckle contrast curve: angular separation vs achievable delta-magnitude.

Separations must be in ascending order.

docs.title: max_detectable_delta_mag(separation_arcsec)
docs.text:
Interpolate the contrast limit at a given angular separation.

Returns 0.0 if separation is below the inner working angle, and the outermost value if beyond the outer boundary.

docs.title: PeriodSpec
docs.text:
Orbital period specification: a scalar (days) or (min, max) range.

Runtime callers may also pass list[float] — existing validation code accepts it — but the public type signature uses this narrower alias.

docs.title: ValidationEngine
docs.text:
Stateless compute core. Receives fully-prepared inputs and executes scenario likelihood calculations without any I/O.

Stateless computation core for TRICERATOPS+ candidate validation.

All external service calls are injected and mockable. The engine holds no mutable state -- safe for concurrent calls.

docs.title: compute_prepared(prepared)
docs.text:
Provider-free compute entrypoint.

Accepts a fully-materialised PreparedValidationInputs and delegates to the existing _compute() logic. No providers are called; all required data must already be present in the prepared payload.

This is the correct entrypoint for remote execution (e.g. Modal workers) where no provider access is available.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default prepared PreparedValidationInputs

Fully-materialised validation inputs. Must have trilegal_population already populated if any trilegal-dependent scenarios are being run.

required

Returns:

Type Description ValidationResult

ValidationResult with FPP, NFPP, and per-scenario results.

docs.title: Protocols
docs.text: Provider interfaces used for dependency injection. Implement these protocols to supply custom data sources to the validation pipeline.

docs.title: StarCatalogProvider
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Protocol for querying stars near a target from a catalog.

docs.title: query_nearby_stars(tic_id, search_radius_px, mission)
docs.text:
Return all catalogued stars within search_radius_px of the target.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default tic_id int

TIC ID (or KIC/EPIC ID for Kepler/K2).

required search_radius_px int

Search radius in pixels.

required mission str

Survey mission name. Only "TESS" is supported for prepared compute. Kepler/K2 support is experimental.

required

Returns:

Type Description StellarField

StellarField with target at index 0.

docs.title: ApertureProvider
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Protocol for retrieving pixel-level cutout images.

docs.title: get_cutouts(ra_deg, dec_deg, size_px, sectors, mission)
docs.text:
Return a list of 2D pixel arrays, one per sector/quarter/campaign.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ra_deg float

Right ascension of the target in degrees.

required dec_deg float

Declination of the target in degrees.

required size_px int

Size of the cutout in pixels (square).

required sectors ndarray

Array of sector/quarter/campaign numbers.

required mission str

Survey mission name. Only "TESS" is fully supported; non-TESS missions raise NotImplementedError in the default provider.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[ndarray]

List of 2D arrays of shape (size_px, size_px). One per sector.

docs.title: PopulationSynthesisProvider
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Protocol for querying background star populations.

docs.title: query(ra_deg, dec_deg, target_tmag, cache_path=None, *, status_callback=None)
docs.text:
Return a background star population for the given sky position.

Raises:

Type Description TRILEGALQueryError

If the query fails.

docs.title: EphemerisResolver
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Protocol for resolving a target string to a ResolvedTarget.

docs.title: Validation Internals
docs.text: Lower-level types for the prepare/compute split.

docs.title: PreparedValidationInputs
docs.text:
Fully-materialised inputs for a provider-free validation compute.

All fields required for ValidationEngine.compute_prepared() are present on this object. No network access, no filesystem IO, and no provider instantiation should happen during or after construction.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: target_id: TIC (or KIC/EPIC) identifier for the validation target. stellar_field: Assembled field including target and neighbours, with flux_ratio and transit_depth_required already computed on each Star. light_curve: Phase-folded, normalised photometric time series. config: Runtime configuration (n_mc_samples, n_best_samples, …). period_days: Orbital period in days. May be a scalar or [min, max] range. trilegal_population: Materialised TRILEGAL background population. Must be provided for scenarios that require it (BTP, BEB, BEBx2P). Pass None if those scenarios are not being run. external_lcs: Ground-based follow-up light curves. None if not available. contrast_curve: AO/speckle contrast curve. None if not available. molusc_data: Pre-loaded MOLUSC companion population data. None if no MOLUSC file was provided. Loaded at prep time by ValidationPreparer or ValidationWorkspace; no filesystem path crosses the compute boundary. scenario_ids: The scenario subset that was prepared for. None means the full default registry. compute_prepared() passes this directly to compute(scenario_ids=...), keeping the prepare/compute contract consistent: the engine runs exactly the scenarios that were prepared.

docs.title: validate()
docs.text:
Preflight validation: assert all scientific preconditions are met.

Called by ValidationEngine.compute_prepared() before any scenario work begins. May also be called explicitly to validate a directly-constructed or deserialized payload.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If structural field invariants are broken (delegated to StellarField.validate()).

UnsupportedComputeModeError

If the mission is not "TESS". Kepler/K2 prepared compute is not yet supported.

PreparedInputIncompleteError

If a required field is missing (no stellar_params, missing TRILEGAL population).

ValidationInputError

If a scientific input value is invalid (empty or inconsistent LightCurve, non-positive period).

docs.title: ValidationPreparer
docs.text:
Validates pre-assembled inputs and produces PreparedValidationInputs.

Pure validation — no I/O, no provider calls. Accepts AssembledInputs from the assembly layer and produces PreparedValidationInputs ready for provider-free compute via ValidationEngine.compute_prepared().

docs.title: __init__(registry=None)
docs.text:
Construct with an optional scenario registry.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default registry ScenarioRegistry | None

Scenario registry used to validate scenario_ids. Must be the same registry passed to the ValidationEngine that will call compute_prepared(). Defaults to DEFAULT_REGISTRY.

None

docs.title: prepare(assembled, config, period_days, *, scenario_ids=None)
docs.text:
Validate pre-assembled domain inputs and produce compute-ready inputs.

Pure validation — no I/O, no provider calls. Validates scientific and structural preconditions, then maps fields from AssembledInputs into PreparedValidationInputs.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default assembled AssembledInputs

Domain inputs from the assembly layer.

required config Config

Runtime compute configuration.

required period_days PeriodSpec

Orbital period (scalar or [min, max] range).

required scenario_ids list[ScenarioID] | None

Optional scenario filter. All must be registered.

None

Returns:

Type Description PreparedValidationInputs

PreparedValidationInputs ready for ValidationEngine.compute_prepared().

Raises:

Type Description UnsupportedComputeModeError

If stellar field mission is not TESS.

PreparedInputIncompleteError

If stellar params are missing.

ValidationInputError

If light curve or period is invalid.

ValueError

If scenario_ids contains unregistered IDs, or if stellar field structural invariants are violated.

docs.title: Error Types
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base class for all TRICERATOPS+ validation errors.

Bases: ValidationError

A required field is missing or None in PreparedValidationInputs.

Examples: no stellar_params on the target star; no trilegal_population when a TRILEGAL-dependent scenario is being run.

Bases: ValidationError

A scientific input value is invalid or internally inconsistent.

Examples: non-positive period_days; empty or shape-mismatched LightCurve arrays.

Bases: ValidationError

The requested compute mode or configuration is not supported.

Bases: ValidationError

A failure occurred during the provider-backed preparation phase.

Wrap provider/IO exceptions in this so callers can distinguish retryable prep failures (network, quota) from non-retryable input errors (bad stellar_params, wrong period sign).

docs.title: ValidationWorkspace
docs.text:
The main user-facing API. Owns I/O providers and orchestrates the full prepare-then-compute workflow.

Stateful analysis session for a single TRICERATOPS+ validation target.

Construction is side-effect-free: the catalog query is deferred until the stellar field is first accessed (via .stars, .target, or .fetch_catalog()). Pass a pre-built stellar_field to skip the catalog query entirely.

Only mission="TESS" is supported for prepared compute. Kepler/K2 support is experimental; passing a non-TESS mission will raise UnsupportedComputeModeError when compute_probs() is called.

docs.title: stars property
docs.text: All stars in the stellar field (target at index 0).

docs.title: target property
docs.text: The target star.

docs.title: stars_df property
docs.text: All stars in the stellar field as a pandas DataFrame.

docs.title: results property
docs.text: Most recent validation result, or None if not yet computed.

docs.title: fpp property
docs.text: False Positive Probability from the most recent run.

docs.title: nfpp property
docs.text: Nearby False Positive Probability from the most recent run.

docs.title: fetch_catalog()
docs.text:
Explicitly fetch the stellar field from the catalog.

Triggers the catalog query if not already loaded. Returns the StellarField. Subsequent calls return the cached field.

This is the explicit alternative to the lazy-on-first-access pattern. Use it when you want to control exactly when the network call happens.

docs.title: add_star(star)
docs.text:
Add a neighbor star to the stellar field. Invalidates cached results.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If a star with the same TIC ID already exists.

docs.title: remove_star(tic_id)
docs.text:
Remove a star by TIC ID. Invalidates cached results.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If tic_id is the target star.

ValueError

If no star with tic_id is found.

docs.title: update_star(tic_id, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Update fields on a star by TIC ID. Invalidates cached results.

Accepts both direct Star attribute names and TIC-style aliases

Teff -> stellar_params.teff_k mass -> stellar_params.mass_msun logg -> stellar_params.logg metallicity -> stellar_params.metallicity_dex

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If no star with tic_id is found.

TypeError

If an alias update is requested and stellar_params is None.

AttributeError

If an unknown attribute name is given.

docs.title: resolve_target(target)
docs.text:
Resolve a target string (e.g. TOI number) using the injected resolver.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If no ephemeris_resolver was provided.

LightCurveError

If resolution fails (propagated from resolver).

docs.title: set_resolved_target(target)
docs.text:
Bind a resolved target to the workspace without performing I/O.

This is intended for higher-level orchestration layers which have already resolved a target and need the workspace assembly path to use that result.

docs.title: calc_depths(transit_depth, pixel_coords_per_sector, aperture_pixels_per_sector, sigma_psf_px=0.75)
docs.text:
Compute flux ratios and intrinsic transit depths for all stars.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default transit_depth float

Observed transit depth (fractional; 0.01 = 1%).

required pixel_coords_per_sector list[ndarray]

Per-sector star pixel positions, each shape (N_stars, 2) as (col, row).

required aperture_pixels_per_sector list[ndarray]

Per-sector aperture pixel positions, each shape (N_pixels, 2) as (col, row).

required sigma_psf_px float

PSF sigma in pixels (default 0.75).

0.75

docs.title: prepare(light_curve, period_days, scenario_ids=None, external_lcs=None, contrast_curve=None, molusc_data=None)
docs.text:
Assemble all I/O inputs and validate them for compute.

This method performs all network and disk I/O: - TRILEGAL background population fetch (if not cached) - Any other provider-backed data assembly

The returned PreparedValidationInputs is fully self-contained and can be inspected, serialized, or passed to compute_prepared().

Returns:

Type Description PreparedValidationInputs

PreparedValidationInputs ready for engine.compute_prepared().

Raises:

Type Description UnsupportedComputeModeError

If mission is not TESS.

PreparedInputIncompleteError

If stellar params are missing.

ValidationInputError

If light curve or period is invalid.

docs.title: compute_prepared(prepared)
docs.text:
Run provider-free compute on pre-assembled inputs.

No network or disk I/O occurs in this method.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default prepared PreparedValidationInputs

Output from prepare() or manually constructed.

required

Returns:

Type Description ValidationResult

ValidationResult, also stored internally for property access.

docs.title: compute_probs(light_curve, period_days, scenario_ids=None, external_lcs=None, contrast_curve=None, molusc_data=None)
docs.text:
Run validation computation and cache the result.

Convenience method that calls prepare() then compute_prepared(). Equivalent to::

prepared = self.prepare(light_curve, period_days, ...)
result = self.compute_prepared(prepared)

Returns:

Type Description ValidationResult

ValidationResult, also stored internally for property access.

docs.title: plot_field(**kwargs)
docs.text:
Plot the stellar field around the target.

Delegates to :func:triceratops.plotting.plot_field.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default **kwargs Any

Forwarded to plot_field (e.g. save, fname).

{}

docs.title: plot_fits(**kwargs)
docs.text:
Plot best-fit model light curves for each non-negligible scenario.

Delegates to :func:triceratops.plotting.plot_fits.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If compute_probs() has not been called yet.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default **kwargs Any

Forwarded to plot_fits (e.g. save, fname).

{}

docs.title: plot_fits_palomar(external_lc_index=0, **kwargs)
docs.text: Plot best-fit models against one stored external light curve.

docs.title: plot_fits_joint(external_lc_index=0, **kwargs)
docs.text: Plot TESS and external best-fit models together.
